stonetop.summary: Tor walking the world in disguise, testing mortals with a challenge and a missing finger.

system.concept: @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-journal.JournalEntry.UoAk5JtClo9sjL1O]{Tor} walking the world in disguise, testing mortals with a challenge and a missing finger.
system.description:
Many tales tell of a stranger met in an unlikely place. The stranger is in some sort of pickle, or challenges our hero, or presents a tempting opportunity. Whatever else happens, the stranger ends up vanishing — but not before our hero notices his missing finger, and realizes that they just met @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-journal.JournalEntry.UoAk5JtClo9sjL1O]{Tor}. Rise to the occasion and you win a gift: a treasure, a secret, a prophecy, or some useful advice. Come up short, and you wind up cursed, or 'win' some treasure that brings only ruin and woe.

When you introduce the nine-fingered stranger, pick or roll a manifestation and the challenge he presents. Consider, too, what gift or curse he might dispense: an arcanum, an answer, a bit of advice, a blessing (e.g. hold 3 Preparation, per the Bolster move), a curse (a threat, a magical affliction), etc.

1d6 manifestation

RollManifestation
1A brash youth
2A formidable warrior
3A put-upon father
4A wealthy merchant
5A mysterious/cagey traveler
6A hapless old man

1d6 challenge

RollChallenge
1A test of might (a duel, a wrestling match, a beast to slay, etc.)
2A test of prowess (a race, a climb, an archery contest, etc.)
3A test of endurance (a drinking contest, a long trudge, an all-night watch, etc.)
4A test of courage (a terror to face, a fate to accept, an injustice to prevent, etc.)
5A test of honor (a chance to steal, or cheat, or rig a bet, etc.)
6A test of kindness (someone wretched to help, some wrong to put right, etc.)
